fields.System.Title: FE App initial config and infra
Phase 1 Case Intake (POC)
fields.System.Description: This feature establishes the foundation of the Case Workflow in Phase 1 by validating that core intake steps, eligibility verification, treatment selection, and rule execution can function together as a single, coherent flow, while intentionally deferring clinical review, protocol logic, and advanced authorization behavior to later phases.

fields.System.Title: Initial Azure Infrastructure for BE (core and Services)

[V1.R1.] Member Search, Selection, & Coverage Determination, including Draft Case
fields.System.Description: PRD

fields.System.Title: [V1.R1.] Diagnosis Selection & Details for Case
fields.System.Description: PRD

fields.System.Title: [V1.R2.]  Patient Clinical Information and Supporting Details
Auto-Determination Logic
fields.System.Description: Evaluates a submitted case to determine whether it qualifies for full system auto-determination based on OH auto-approval logic, payer drug configurations (AP/PDL),  and health plan business rules. This evaluation establishes whether the case can be completed without manual clinical or operational intervention.

fields.System.Title: Queues Management
fields.System.Description: Routes cases requiring manual review to the appropriate operational queue (CRS, Nursing, PharmD, MD) based on OncoHealth workqueue rules, clinical complexity, and payer-specific assignment logic, ensuring workload visibility and auditability.

fields.System.Title: NMI for Clinical Data & Medical Records
fields.System.Description:
• Send NMI for Clinical Data and Medical Records
  • Track NMI Status and Due Dates
  • Receive and Associate Clinical Documents to Case
  • Resume Case Review After NMI Response

fields.System.Title: Provider Intervention (P2P / CCF)
fields.System.Description:

  • Capture Provider Response and Outcome
  • Update Case and Complete Clinical Determination

fields.System.Title: Medical Drug Authorization Outbound (Health Plan Integration)
Tech Debt
fields.System.Description:
DAL

  • Introduce Unit of Work pattern (Remove SaveChangesAsync() calls from repository methods) (done)

  • Add .AsNoTracking() to all read-only queries

  • Configure a global query filter for ISoftDelete entities. Remove manual soft-delete checks from repository methods

  • Remove Mapper dependency from repository layer. Mapping should be done outside repository level

  • Remove IDisposable from repository base class (done)

  • Convert RepositoryBase into an abstract base class and refactor entity-specific repositories (e.g., CaseRepository) to inherit from it, removing redundant context fields and keeping only entity-specific logic where needed

  • Missing CancellationToken Propagation
  • Timezone issue (Should use DateTime.UtcNow for consistency with other code that uses UTC)
  • Repositories storing _ctx again are unnecessary
  • Fragile casting chain in mappers return (T)(IDto<Case>)new CaseDTO
  • In-memory filtering after loading all data (check all places)
  • Use AddAsync() only when using value generators that access the database, 99% need to use Add() since REAL async happens in await _ctx.SaveChangesAsync() (done)
  • Fix namespaces in DAL.Model. Screenshot attached 
  • agree on using fluent API or data annotation on entity models. See example of [Foreing Key] vs HasForeignKey(x => x.Version) OnModelCreating. In EF fluent Api has priority #1

fields.System.Title: Database Administration
fields.System.Description:
Instance & Configuration

  • Install and configure PostgreSQL instance on Azure
  • Define and document postgresql.conf baseline settings (memory, connections, WAL, autovacuum)
  • Configure pg_hba.conf for client authentication
  • Set up and configure PgBouncer for connection pooling
  • Define and enforce naming conventions across schemas, tables, and columns

Schema & Object Management

  • Create domain schemas (eligibility, provider, payer, cases)
  • Set up Alembic / SQLAlchemy as the migration tool
  • Define migration review and approval process
  • [ Deveops team ] Build Azure Devops for gated migration execution
  • Revoke DDL privileges from application user in production
  • Create and document schema change request process for backend team

Security

  • Define and implement role and privilege model (app user, migrations user, read-only user, admin)
  • Configure SSL/TLS for all connections
  • Set up pgAudit for access and change auditing
  • Implement secrets management for database credentials (Azure Key Vault or similar)
  • Define and schedule access review process
  • Configure row-level security where needed

Backup & Recovery

  • Define RPO and RTO targets
  • Implement backup strategy (pg_basebackup + WAL archiving via pgBackRest or WAL-G)
  • Schedule and automate backups via Airflow
  • Document and test restore procedures
  • Set up backup verification checks as Airflow DAGs

High Availability & Replication

  • Configure streaming replication (primary + standby)
  • Set up automatic failover (Patroni or Azure-managed HA)
  • Document planned failover and switchover runbooks
  • Monitor replication lag via alerting

Performance Monitoring & Tuning

  • Enable and configure pg_stat_statements
  • Set up slow query logging and alerting
  • Define autovacuum tuning baseline
  • Build index review process in coordination with backend team
  • Schedule regular bloat monitoring (table and index bloat)
  • Set up lock contention monitoring and alerting

Capacity Planning

  • Define disk, CPU, memory, and connection usage baselines
  • Set up alerting thresholds for all key metrics
  • Build capacity forecasting process as data grows across clinical, eligibility, and provider domains

Tooling & Stack

  • Set up SQLAlchemy Core as standard Python database access library
  • Agree and document Dapper as backend team's data access layer (no EF ORM)
  • Integrate Liquibase or Alembic into CI/CD pipeline
  • Configure Airflow connections and variables for all database environments
  • Set up database monitoring tooling (pgAdmin, pgBadger, or Datadog Postgres integration)

Documentation & Governance

  • Maintain data dictionary for all schemas and tables
  • Document all runbooks for common operational tasks (failover, restore, slot cleanup, VACUUM)
  • Define and document change management process for DDL changes
  • Define SLAs for database availability and query performance
  • Schedule and document regular access review audits

Incident Response

  • Define on-call ownership and escalation path for database incidents
  • Document common incident runbooks (connection exhaustion, deadlocks, disk full, WAL bloat)
  • Define post-incident review process
